case_title: ANGELA HILL VS. ROBBINS GELLER RUDMAN & DOWD LLP et al

docket_entries.description: NOTICE OF LODGMENT OF EXHIBITS OIN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION FILED BY DEFENDANT ROBBINS GELLER RUDMAN & DOWD LLP

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ROBBINS GELLER RUDMAN & DOWD LLP'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION. A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT IS PROCEDURALLY AND SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE AND SEVERANCE OF THE ONE-SIDED PROVISIONS IS NOT APPROPRIATE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. Ntc Of Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action ; Declaration(S)
Off Calendar Oct-03-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEPHEN R. JAFFE OF THE JAFFE LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 618-0100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. AMY FINDLEY, TEL. NO. (619) 235-1544 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Defendant Robbins Geller Rudman & Dowd LLP'S motion to compel arbitration and stay action is denied. The arbitration agreement is procedurally and substantively unconscionable and severance of the one-sided provisions is not appropriate. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7770 =302/HEK
